ocr.text: The maritime sector remains under constant scrutiny as fluctuations in voyage charter revenue and vessel age profiles
significantly impact industry dynamics. As highlighted in the accompanying table, the CAGR(5-Year) for voyage charter
revenue illustrates an impressive growth trajectory, with a notable increase from CHF120M to CHF180M over the period. This
growth reflects a robust demand for maritime transport services, particularly in the context of an overall trend categorized as
Stable.
Year  RevenueTrends  GrowthRate
2019  CHF120M  5.0%  Stable
2020  CHF130M  8.3%  Increase
2021  [HIDDEN]  15.4%  RapidGrowth
2022  CHF165M  10.0%  Sustained
2023  [HIDDEN]  9.1%  Consistent
CAGR(5-Year)  CHF145M  8.6%  OverallTrend
However, the age profile of the fleet presents challenges, with a substantial proportion of vessels, specifically those aged
between 11-15 years, accounting for 4.5% of the total fleet value. This statistic raises concerns regarding the sustainability and
operational efficiency of older vessels, which are increasingly subject to scrapping considerations.
VesselType  VesselAge(Years)  ScrappingRate(%)  FleetSize  TotalRevenue(CHF)  NetIncome(CHF)  CAPEX(CHF)  AgeProfile
ContainerShips  0-5  2.5  100  50M  15M  0-5  BulkCarriers  6-10
11-15  GeneralCargo  16-20  5.0  50  20M  4M  16-20  AllVessels  0-5  2.8
OlderVessels  Over25  7.0  10  5M  1M  Over25  ScrapValue(CHF)  EstimatedCosts  25M  2M
TotalFleetValue  MarketValue(CHF)  650M  NetWorth(CHF)  300M  ProjectedGrowth(%)  5-YearCAGR  [HIDDEN]  ScrappingGrowth(%)  3.0  Total
Notably, the data reveals that the total fleet value is influenced by the aging fleet, with older vessels, particularly those
generating CHF150M in revenue, increasingly at risk due to maintenance costs and regulatory pressures. Furthermore, the
average age of vessels in the fleet is critical, with 3.0 years marked as a pivotal threshold for operational viability. The insights
derived from this data are essential for stakeholders aiming to navigate the complexities of the shipping market while ensuring
fleet competitiveness and compliance with evolving maritime regulations.
